description: Farmers, herders, and those who work the land and sea.
jobs: Farmer
A sun-weathered figure plowing fields and sowing seed from dawn to dusk.
Shepherd
A wind-bitten wanderer guiding sheep to fresh pasture with crook and dog.
Cowherd
Mud-caked and patient, driving cattle between pastures and watching over spring calving.
Swineherd
A rough-clothed herder leading pigs into the forest to root for acorns.
Goatherd
Sure-footed and sunburnt, scrambling up rocky slopes to keep goats out of the crops.
Falconer
A leather-gloved handler training hawks on the fist and hunting game birds on the wing.
Huntsman
A quiet figure tracking deer and boar through deep woods with hounds and bow.
Gamekeeper
A stern warden patrolling the lord's forest, guarding game and prosecuting poachers.
Forester
An axe-carrying woodsman marking timber and managing coppice through the seasons.
Woodcutter
A broad-shouldered worker felling trees with axe and saw, then splitting trunks into logs.
Miller
A flour-dusted figure grinding grain between heavy stones powered by wind or water.
Vintner
Purple-stained hands crushing grapes and tending fermenting barrels into finished wine.
Fisherman
A salt-crusted mariner hauling nets from a rocking boat and bringing the catch to market.
Fisher
Barefoot and patient, wading streams and casting lines for freshwater fish.
Beekeeper
A veiled tender of straw skeps, harvesting golden honey and pale beeswax.
Herbalist
A keen-eyed gatherer drying wild plants into bundles for teas, salves, and poultices.
Orchardist
Bark-scratched arms pruning and grafting fruit trees from blossom through harvest.
Vintager
A stooped picker pulling ripe grapes from the vine during the autumn crush.
Thresher
Dust-caked and rhythmic, beating sheaves with a flail to separate grain from chaff.
Reaper
A sickle-swinging laborer cutting standing wheat close to the ground under hot sun.
Plowman
A mud-splattered worker walking behind ox and plow, turning soil into spring furrows.
Hayward
A watchful guardian of common fields, keeping stray livestock out of the crops.
Granary Keeper
A careful steward measuring grain into dry bins and guarding stores against rats.
Livestock Dealer
A sharp-eyed haggler buying, selling, and bartering cattle at crowded market fairs.
Kennel Master
A weathered handler keeping the lord's hounds fed, trained, and straining at the leash.
Poacher
A furtive shadow sneaking onto noble land at night to snare forbidden game.
Honey Gatherer
A daring climber scaling trees and cliffs to rob wild bee colonies of their honey.
Mushroom Gatherer
A crouching forager searching damp forest floors, knowing which fungi feed and which kill.
Pearl Diver
A deep-lunged swimmer plunging into cold water to pry pearls from oyster beds.
Leech Gatherer
Barefoot and wading through marshes, letting leeches latch on for later sale to healers.
Herb Gatherer
A basket-laden wanderer roaming meadows and hedgerows for roots, leaves, and blossoms.
Orc Hunter
A scarred tracker ambushing bands of savage humanoids before they raid settlements.
Cattle Driver
Dust-choked and tireless, pushing herds down long roads to distant market towns.
Sheep Driver
A dog-flanked figure moving large flocks across open country to seasonal grazing.
Goat Herder
A wiry hill-dweller keeping a mixed flock fed and milked on scrubby pasture.
Pig Farmer
A slop-spattered raiser of hogs, fattening them on scraps and grain for slaughter.
Chicken Farmer
An early riser collecting eggs at dawn and raising chickens for meat and feathers.
Duck Farmer
A pond-side keeper raising ducks for eggs, meat, and soft down feathers.
Goose Herder
A stick-waving figure driving flocks of geese to pasture and plucking feathers for quills.
Turkey Raiser
A yard-bound farmer breeding and fattening turkeys for feast-day tables.
Rabbit Breeder
A quiet keeper of warrens, selling meat and soft pelts at the local market.
Deer Keeper
A vigilant steward maintaining a fenced park and managing the herd for the lord's hunt.
Wild Boar Hunter
A spear-braced hunter cornering tusked boar with dogs in the deep forest.
Bear Hunter
A heavy-trap setter tracking bears through mountain country for hide and bounty.
Wolf Hunter
A grim specialist hunting wolf packs with poison bait, pit traps, and organized drives.
Fox Hunter
A red-coated rider chasing foxes with hounds across fields and hedgerows.
Bird Catcher
A lime-fingered trapper spreading sticky sticks in branches to catch songbirds alive.
Fowler
A marsh-wading hunter of wild ducks and geese, armed with bow, net, and decoy.
Trapper
A fur-clad loner setting steel-jawed traps along animal trails in the deep woods.
Snare Setter
Quiet hands tying wire loops along rabbit runs for passive catching at dawn.
Horse Trainer
A calm-voiced breaker of young colts, schooling them to saddle, bridle, and rider.
Falcon Trainer
A patient handler manning wild raptors on the fist and teaching them the lure.
